name: Lentils with Chilies, Pork, and Fruit - Lentejas En Adobo
recipeIngredient: 1 lb Boneless stewing pork, cut into 1-inch cubes
5 small Chilies anchos, cleaned of veins and seeds
1 Garlic clove, peeled and roughly chopped
1/4 tsp Dried oregano, Mexican if possible
1 tbsp Melted lard or safflower oil
1 medium Plantain (about 8-oz), peeled and cut into 1/4 inch cubes
2 Thick pineapple slices, peeled, cored, and cut into small triangular wedges
recipeInstructions:
The lentils: Run the lentils through your hands to ensure there are no stones or other foreign bodies. Rinse them in two changes of water and place in a pan. Add onion, salt to taste, and enough water to come about 2 inches above the surface of the lentils. Set over medium heat and bring to a fast simmer. Continue simmering until the lentils are quite soft (~3 hours), depending on their age. Keep a pan of near-boiling water ready for adding if necessary.
Put the pork pieces into a separate pan; add salt to taste and water to cover. Bring to a fast simmer and continue simmering until the pork is tender but not soft (about 25 minutes). Strain, reserving the broth, and set broth and meat aside.
Cover the dried chilies with boiling water and soak for about 15 minutes until softened and fleshy. Drain and put into a blender with 1 cup of reserved pork broth, broiled tomatoes, garlic, oregano, clove, and cinnamon; blend until smooth, adding more broth if needed to release the blades.
Heat the lard in a small frying pan, add blended ingredients, and fry over medium heat, stirring and scraping the bottom. Cook until reduced and well seasoned (~4 minutes). Add to lentils and pork; simmer together with remaining broth, plantain, and pineapple for about 30 minutes. Adjust salt and water as needed. The mixture should be like a thick soup.
